theme: Nova staging environment data leak into production plus Atlas sprint mid-point review with delivery risk and Vertex onboarding a new senior engineer

events.text: CRITICAL: staging test data appearing in production — 3 enterprise customers reporting sandbox user records in their production dashboards
NOVA-301 Critical — staging data leaked into production, 3 customers affected: Triflex, Maybro, Solveig
Infra: staging and production DBs sharing connection pool since last night's infra config deploy at 22:14
REVERT: db-connection-pool.py — staging and production pool config merged incorrectly in last night's deploy
NOVA-302 — connection pool revert deployed, staging and production DB connections separating
CTO: connection pool reverted — how many staging records leaked into production?
test: audit production DB for staging-origin records — identify all rows inserted since 22:14 with staging tenant IDs
DBA: querying production DB for staging tenant IDs — checking users, events, feature_flags tables
NOVA-303 opened — production DB audit for staging records since 22:14
DBA: 1,847 staging records found in production — 1,204 in events table, 412 in users table, 231 in feature_flags
NOVA-303 update — 1,847 staging records in production: events 1,204, users 412, feature_flags 231
Engineering: running purge script for all 1,847 staging records from production — staging tenant IDs flagged
fix: purge staging records from production — delete all rows with staging tenant IDs across 3 tables
NOVA-304 — purge of 1,847 staging records in progress
Purge progress: 800 of 1,847 records removed — events table clean, users and feature_flags in progress
Sprint mid-point review started — 11 of 23 tasks complete, 4 at risk, 8 not started
ATL-301 sprint mid-point — 11 complete, 4 at risk, 8 not started, sprint velocity 74%
PM: 4 at-risk tasks all depend on Rajesh — SSO integration, audit trail, RBAC enforcement, reporting module
Rajesh OOO message found — emergency family leave since Monday, no handoff notes left for 4 dependent tasks

PM escalating to engineering lead — 4 unassigned tasks, sprint delivery at risk, need emergency reallocation
Subject: Sprint delivery risk — Rajesh OOO | Body: 4 tasks unassigned due to emergency leave, requesting engineering lead to assess reallocation
ATL-303 escalation — engineering lead reviewing 4 unassigned tasks for reallocation
Purge complete — all 1,847 staging records removed from production at 11:07
NOVA-304 closed — 1,847 staging records purged from production
DBA: running integrity check on production DB — confirming zero staging tenant IDs remain
test: post-purge integrity check — zero staging tenant IDs in production across all 3 tables
Integrity check passed — zero staging records in production, all 3 tables clean
NOVA-305 integrity check passed — production clean
Arnav starts today — senior engineer joining from Razorpay, 6 years payments infrastructure experience
VTX-201 — Arnav onboarding day 1, senior engineer, ex-Razorpay payments infra
Engineering lead: Arnav's onboarding plan — day 1 setup, day 2-3 codebase walkthrough, day 4-5 first task on billing engine
Arnav onboarding — architecture walkthrough with CTO at 14:00 today
PM: notifying 3 affected customers — Triflex, Maybro, Solveig each getting direct call explaining staging data visibility
Subject: Data visibility incident — Triflex | Body: Staging test data briefly visible in your dashboard 22:14-11:07, all records removed, no production data was accessed
Subject: Data visibility incident — Maybro | Body: Staging test data briefly visible in your dashboard 22:14-11:07, all records removed
Subject: Data visibility incident — Solveig | Body: Staging test data briefly visible in your dashboard 22:14-11:07, all records removed
NOVA-306 — all 3 customers notified with incident explanation
Engineering lead assessment complete — SSO and audit trail can be handled by Meera (2 days each), RBAC needs external contractor or scope reduction
ATL-304 reallocation plan — SSO to Meera, audit trail to Meera, RBAC decision needed, reporting descoped to next sprint
PM: client informed of Rajesh OOO — discussing RBAC scope: defer to sprint +1 or reduce scope to read-only RBAC only
Subject: Sprint delivery update — RBAC scope | Body: Key engineer OOO, requesting client input: defer RBAC to sprint +1 or reduce to read-only RBAC in current sprint
ATL-305 — client RBAC scope decision pending
Root cause confirmed — infra config deploy at 22:14 used same connection string env var name in staging and production Helm chart
NOVA-307 root cause — Helm chart env var collision: DATABASE_URL used in both staging and production namespaces
fix: rename staging DB env var to STAGING_DATABASE_URL in all Helm charts — prevents namespace collision
NOVA-308 fix — env var renamed, staging and production configs now distinct
Env var fix deployed — staging uses STAGING_DATABASE_URL, production uses PRODUCTION_DATABASE_URL, collision impossible
Arnav dev environment setup complete — repo cloned, dependencies installed, local services running in 48 minutes
VTX-202 — Arnav dev env setup complete, ahead of expected 2-hour setup time
Arnav: reviewed billing engine codebase — identifying legacy Stripe v1 integration that matches his Razorpay experience
Client response on RBAC scope — accepting read-only RBAC in current sprint, full RBAC in sprint +1
ATL-305 closed — RBAC scoped to read-only in current sprint, full RBAC sprint +1
Meera starting SSO integration — picking up Rajesh's task from last known state in JIRA
ATL-306 In Progress — Meera assigned to SSO integration, reviewing Rajesh's progress notes
Meera: Rajesh had SSO 60% complete — SAML assertion parsing done, session creation and logout flow remaining
ATL-306 update — SSO 60% complete by Rajesh, session creation and logout remaining
Triflex CSM called — their CTO wants written incident report by EOD, specifically asking about data isolation architecture
NOVA-309 — Triflex requesting written incident report by EOD, CTO asking about data isolation

docs: staging-to-production data leak incident report — root cause Helm env var collision, timeline, 1,847 records purged, 3 preventive measures
NOVA-310 incident report draft — 3 preventive measures: env var naming convention, DB namespace isolation, nightly isolation check

feat: nightly DB isolation check — scans production for staging tenant ID patterns, alerts on any match
NOVA-311 — nightly DB isolation check scheduled, fires 02:00 daily
Engineering lead presenting Arnav with codebase map — 4 domains: billing, auth, data pipeline, notification

feat: SSO session JWT generation — creates session token from validated SAML assertion, matching Rajesh's assertion parser

feat: Helm chart env var collision lint — CI check flags duplicate env var names across staging and production namespaces

Arnav completed codebase walkthrough of billing domain — deep understanding of payment retry, invoicing, and reconciliation flows

Arnav's retry refactor proposal: replace fixed 3-retry with exponential backoff + jitter — reduces payment failure rate by estimated 18%
VTX-205 — Arnav retry refactor proposal: exponential backoff + jitter, estimated 18% payment failure rate reduction
CTO incident report first draft complete — 4 sections: timeline, root cause, impact, preventive measures
NOVA-310 update — incident report first draft, CTO reviewing technical accuracy

docs: incident report v2 — adding DB namespace isolation architecture diagram and connection pool config
NOVA-310 update — architecture diagram added to incident report

feat: SSO logout flow — JWT invalidation, session clear, IdP logout redirect

feat: payment retry exponential backoff — replaces fixed 3-retry with jitter-based exponential schedule

Arnav's retry prototype complete — 50 synthetic payment failures tested, 0 with exponential backoff + jitter vs 8 with fixed retry
Prototype result: 84% fewer payment failures in test suite — exponential backoff + jitter working exactly as predicted
VTX-206 prototype result — 84% fewer payment failures in 50-event test suite
test: payment retry prototype — 50 synthetic failures, exponential backoff vs fixed retry comparison

DB namespace isolation enforced in Kubernetes — staging and production now in separate namespaces with network policy blocking cross-namespace DB access
feat: Kubernetes namespace isolation — network policy blocks staging from accessing production DB namespace
NOVA-314 — K8s network policy deployed, staging-to-production DB access structurally impossible
CTO: K8s network policy is the real fix — even if env vars collide again, network layer blocks the connection
CTO: Arnav's 14:00 walkthrough agenda updated — billing retry refactor + broader architecture discussion on payment resilience
VTX-207 — 14:00 walkthrough agenda: retry refactor, payment resilience architecture discussion
